spots.name: Piazza San Marco
spots.description: The grand square at the heart of Venice — arrive early to beat the crowds.

spots.name: Rialto Bridge
spots.description: The oldest bridge spanning the Grand Canal, lined with shops and views.

spots.name: Doge's Palace
spots.description: Gothic masterpiece revealing centuries of Venetian power and artistry.

spots.name: Caffè Florian
spots.description: The oldest café in Italy, serving coffee since 1720 under gilded ceilings.

spots.name: Torrefazione Cannaregio
spots.description: Local favorite for authentic espresso away from the tourist trail.

spots.name: Caffè del Doge
spots.description: Specialty roaster with a cozy spot near Rialto — try the cold brew.

spots.name: Trattoria alla Madonna
spots.description: Classic Venetian seafood restaurant near the Rialto, unchanged for decades.

spots.name: Osteria Alle Testiere
spots.description: Tiny, intimate restaurant with some of the freshest fish in Venice.

spots.name: Dal Moro's Fresh Pasta to Go
spots.description: Quick, affordable fresh pasta — surprisingly excellent for a takeaway spot.

spots.name: Libreria Acqua Alta
spots.description: A surreal bookshop where books are stored in bathtubs and gondolas.

spots.name: Burano Island
spots.description: Vibrantly painted fishing village — a photographer's dream just 40 minutes away.

spots.name: Scala Contarini del Bovolo
spots.description: Hidden Renaissance spiral staircase with a stunning rooftop view.
